title: 9 Selling Foreign Currency to a Customer
body_text:
9	Selling Foreign Currency to a Customer
9.1	Overview of Selling Foreign Currency to a Customer
To sell foreign currency to a customer, a Standard FX Notes user enters the order on FX Notes. Globex 2000 receives the order, then sends the foreign currency to your financial institution via courier or armoured car, depending on the amount. Foreign currency can only be purchased in Canadian dollars.
Caution
:	If cash is received from a customer to pay for foreign currency, a FINTRAC Large Cash Transaction Report must be submitted if the amount is $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more. Contact your AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
9.2	Available Currencies
There are two categories of foreign currency notes: major currencies and minor currencies. The following table lists major currencies. Those not included in the table are considered minor currencies.
Note:	The list of major currencies is subject to change. Any changes will be communicated within the FX Notes application.
Country
Currency
Australia
Australian Dollar
Europe
Euro
Japan
Yen
Mexico
Mexican Peso
Switzerland
Swiss Franc
United Kingdom
Pound Sterling
United States of America
US Dollar
See Section 11.3, Viewing a List of Available Foreign Currencies and Their Images, for more information.
9.3	Foreign Currency Order Deadlines and Delivery Methods
Foreign currency orders are delivered directly to your financial institution by courier or armoured car, depending on the amount.
Major
currency orders are delivered to major Canadian cities within one to two business days and to the rest of Canada within three to five business days. Minor currency orders, regardless of location, are delivered within one to five business days.
See the table below for order deadlines and delivery times.
Region
Order Deadline
BC
12:00 pm PT
Ontario and Manitoba
3:00 pm ET
Note:	Applicable fees are added when a transaction is processed on FX Notes.
Note:	The courier limit for shipping cash to financial institutions is $10,000 CAD per package, with a maximum of two packages per order. Amounts that exceed the $20,000 CAD limit will be delivered by armoured courier, for which a fee is charged.
If an order is placed on FX Notes after the 3:00 pm ET (12:00 pm PT) deadline, the transaction delivery will be treated as if it were made the next business day. For example, a major currency order that is placed on Monday at 3:00 pm PT will be processed with MondayГўВҖВҷs exchange rate but sent as if the order were made on Tuesday and delivered to your financial institution on Wednesday or Thursday.
Related Information
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
9.4	Selling Foreign Currency to a Customer
To sell foreign currency to a customer, a Standard FX Notes user proceeds as follows:
Log in to FX Notes as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to FX Notes.
Select
Sell to Client
on the home page. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select ГўВҖВңSell to ClientГўВҖВқ
Complete the order form as follows. See Figure 2 and Figure 3.
Figure 2: Order Form ГўВҖВ“ Top of Page
Figure 3: Order Form ГўВҖВ“ Bottom of Page
ГўВ‘
Branch Location
ГўВҖВ“ Select a branch from the Branch Location drop-down menu if you have access to multiple branches. If you only have access to one branch, itГўВҖВҷs displayed in this field by default.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Pricing Options
ГўВҖВ“ Select your foreign exchange spread from the Pricing Options drop-down menu. The options available for selection reflect any foreign exchange spreads you previously set up. See Chapter 7, Managing FX Notes Foreign Exchange Spreads and Transaction Fees.
ГўВ‘Вў
Product Type
ГўВҖВ“ The product type is always CASH. This field isnГўВҖВҷt editable.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Transaction Fee
ГўВҖВ“ Select
Yes
or
No
to include your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs transaction fee. The amount is displayed in the Transaction Fee field in the bottom right-hand corner of the page. Users can add, change, or remove the fee, per internal policy. Your transaction fees are managed on the Retail Rate Modifiers page. See Chapter 7, Managing FX Notes Foreign Exchange Spreads and Transaction Fees.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Currency
ГўВҖВ“ Select a currency from the drop-down menu.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Amount
Enter the exact amount of foreign currency that the customer wants to purchase (e.g., ГўВӮВ¬2,000). The Canadian dollar equivalent will show in the CAD Value field.
ГўВ‘ВҰ
Rate
ГўВҖВ“ The Globex 2000 exchange rate is displayed. This field isnГўВҖВҷt editable.
Important:
Rates are held for 15 minutes and can be refreshed at any time.
ГўВ‘В§
CAD Value
ГўВҖВ“Foreign currency CAD equivalent is displayed in the Amount field.
ГўВ‘ВЁ
Verify Currency
ГўВҖВ“ ItГўВҖВҷs not necessary to view the currency when selling to customers. However, if you want to see images of the notes, select
View Currency
.
ГўВ‘В©
Denomination Breakdown
ГўВҖВ“ Select specific denominations in the Denomination Breakdown section. Requests are filled on a best-efforts basis.
ГўВ‘ВӘ
Add Currency ГўВҖВ“
Select
Add Currency
if the customer wants to order multiple foreign currencies. Repeat the steps described above.
ГўВ‘В«
CustomerГўВҖВҷs Name
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the customerГўВҖВҷs reference. This will help you identify which customer ordered the FX Bank Notes.
ГўВ‘В¬
Special Requests / Printable Notes
ГўВҖВ“ Enter any notes or special requests.
ГўВ‘Вӯ
Select
Submit Order.
Select
Confirm
to proceed with the order. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Confirm Order
View the currency order receipt, then print a copy for the customer using the browserГўВҖВҷs print function. The receipt displays the total order amount including the Canadian dollar equivalent, your transaction fee, and the shipping fee. Your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs logo is displayed in the top right-hand corner. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Currency Order Receipt
AML Considerations
Contact your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information on the requirements for foreign exchange transactions. This may include completing a foreign exchange transaction ticket. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use the Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket, Form 1923, and refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for more information.
Related Information
Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket,
Form 1923
(available to Operations Manual Program subscribers only)
Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule,
Form 2725
(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
9.5	Editing and Cancelling Foreign Currency Orders
Foreign currency orders can be edited or cancelled up to one hour after the initial order was made while they are still in Pending Operational Approval status. If an order canГўВҖВҷt be edited or cancelled, keep the foreign currency for your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs inventory or return the currency to GlobexВ 2000 after the order is received. See Chapter 10, Buying Foreign Currency from a Customer, for procedures.
To edit or delete a foreign currency order:
Log in to FX Notes as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to FX Notes.
Select
Transactional
, then
Orders
on the left-hand menu. The most recently submitted order is at the top of the list. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Navigate to Orders
Select the edit icon beside the order that you want to edit.
Important:
You can only edit orders with the status ГўВҖВңPending Operations ApprovalГўВҖВқ. See FigureВ 7.
Figure 7: Select the Edit Icon
To edit the order:
Update the order as needed, then select
Update Order
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Select ГўВҖВңUpdate OrderГўВҖВқ
To cancel the order:
Select
Cancel Order
, then select
Yes, Cancel Order
. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Select ГўВҖВңCancel OrderГўВҖВқ
9.6	Receiving Foreign Currency Orders
The contents of incoming foreign currency parcels must be verified in detail, in joint custody, and within 24 hours of receipt. Verify the number of bank notes received in each denomination, and verify the authenticity (e.g., no counterfeit) of each bank note.
If the value of an incoming foreign currency parcel doesnГўВҖВҷt correspond to the value ordered, immediately contact GlobexВ 2000.
Inspect packages received by the courier for signs of tampering or damage. Look for the following:
Partial or full ripping of the outer/inner package
Re-taping of the outer package (e.g., using packaging tape)
Signs of tampering on the inside envelopes
Missing content
If a damaged package is received, proceed as follows:
Document the incident, providing the delivery time and who made the delivery.
Take photos, showing the damage of the outer and/or inner packaging.
Take a photo of the content (if there are items missing).
Inform Globex 2000 of the incident by email,
C1support@globex2000.com
, and attach photos.
Important
: 	Keep damaged packaging until the incident is reported to Globex 2000 and they provide instructions.
Globex 2000 representatives may request that you ship the damaged packaging to a specified address for further inspection. Globex 2000 representatives will provide you with the shipping address and any other procedures.
END
